chapterSummary.title: Ear to Ear
chapterSummary.content: This chapter from the NCERT Looking Around textbook helps students observe and understand facial features. By looking closely at faces around them, children learn that every person has unique features - from the shape of their eyes and nose to the colour of their hair and skin. The chapter highlights that we inherit features from our parents, which is why family members often look similar. It celebrates the diversity of human appearances and teaches children to respect and appreciate how everyone looks different.
keyPoints: Every face has the same basic features but shaped differently
Eyes, nose, ears, mouth, and hair vary from person to person
We inherit our features from our parents and grandparents
Even identical twins have tiny differences
Special marks like dimples, freckles, and moles add uniqueness
All differences in appearance are natural and should be respected
learningObjectives: Observe and describe different facial features carefully
Identify similarities and differences between faces
Understand that features are inherited from parents
Appreciate the diversity of human appearances
Develop respect for how everyone looks different

curatedNotes.title: Mirror Activity
curatedNotes.content: Look at your face carefully in a mirror. Describe the shape of your eyes, your nose, your ears, and your hair. Now compare with a family member. What features are similar? What is different? Draw your own face showing all the details you notice.

curatedNotes.title: Family Features Chart
curatedNotes.content: Make a table with columns for eye colour, hair type (straight/wavy/curly), dimples (yes/no), and earlobe type (attached/free). Fill it in for yourself, your parents, and your grandparents if you can. Can you see which features you got from which parent?

curatedNotes.title: Respecting Differences
curatedNotes.content: Remember: everyone looks different and that is beautiful! Never make fun of how someone looks. If someone teases you about your appearance, remember that your features make you special. Talk to a trusted adult if someone is unkind about how you or others look.
